vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1326: PAN-1316 follow-up: stopWorkspaceDocker resolves wrong workspace for specialist agents

plan.title: PAN-1316 follow-up: stopWorkspaceDocker resolves wrong workspace for specialist agents
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

references.label: bd7d00787 — fix commit on this branch

narratives.Problem: PR #1317 (PAN-1316) wired stopWorkspaceDocker into POST /api/agents/:id/stop and `pan kill` by reading state.workspace + state.issueId from the agent's own state. That works for work agents (they own the workspace) but not for specialists (review/test/ship), whose state.workspace either points at the orchestrator's workspace or is absent — so killing a specialist silently failed to tear down the right workspace stack. The fix at commit bd7d00787 on this branch now resolves the workspace from the issue via resolveProjectFromIssue + findWorkspacePath (mirroring postMergeLifecycle). The issue was reopened because the test plan from the original issue (specialist kill test, work-agent regression test, manual dashboard stop verification) was never executed — the implementation path is present but unproven by automated tests.
narratives.Proposal: Add three artifacts that prove the fix and lock down the regression: (1) a new src/cli/commands/__tests__/kill.test.ts covering specialist (no state.workspace) and work (state.workspace differs from resolver output) kill paths, asserting findWorkspacePath + stopWorkspaceDocker call arguments; (2) extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts with the equivalent assertions for the HTTP stop handler (matching the route's dynamic-import specifiers in the vi.mock declarations); (3) add docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md as a reproducible playbook for the human verifier — kill a ship specialist via the dashboard and confirm docker ps no longer shows that issue's stack. No production code changes — the fix already shipped.

autoDecisions.summary: Created a new kill.test.ts file rather than extending another CLI test.
autoDecisions.rationale: No kill.test.ts exists today and the new tests assert a focused contract (docker teardown call args). A dedicated file keeps that contract auditable and discoverable.

autoDecisions.summary: Extended the existing agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts for the route case rather than creating a sibling test file.
autoDecisions.rationale: That file already provides the createAgentStopHandler runner, the EventStoreService stub, and the node:fs/promises mock — reusing it minimizes test scaffolding duplication and keeps related agent-stop assertions co-located.

autoDecisions.summary: Assert on stopWorkspaceDocker mock call arguments instead of console output or DockerCleanupResult.containersFound.
autoDecisions.rationale: The contract being verified is 'we resolved the right workspace path from the issue and passed it to stopWorkspaceDocker' — that's the smallest verifiable surface that catches a regression. Side-effect assertions (console logs, container counts) leak implementation detail and can pass even when the resolver is wrong if existsSync happens to return false.

autoDecisions.summary: Work-agent regression case sets state.workspace to a clearly different path from findWorkspacePath's mocked return.
autoDecisions.rationale: If the test used the same value for both, it would pass even if the code reverted to reading state.workspace. Forcing them to diverge proves the new code-path is what's running.

autoDecisions.summary: Captured manual dashboard-stop verification as a docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md recipe rather than driving Playwright from the work agent.
autoDecisions.rationale: An autonomous agent cannot reliably drive the dashboard UI against a live docker stack (workspace must be running, real containers must exist). The unit test in bead 2 provides the code-level equivalent; the recipe gives the human verifier a fixed playbook with expected docker ps before/after evidence for sign-off.

autoDecisions.summary: Three beads (not one larger 'add all tests' bead, not five micro-beads per assertion).
autoDecisions.rationale: Each surface (CLI command, HTTP handler, manual recipe) has its own mock setup and test file. Splitting further would create duplicate-setup beads; combining would make a single bead touch three different files with three different scaffoldings.

Each bead is a mechanically simple test/doc addition with no downstream foundation impact. The fix it covers is already merged on the branch; later beads do not depend on the test scaffolding shape. Typecheck/lint/verification gate will catch a malformed test trivially.

items.title: Add kill.test.ts covering specialist and work-agent docker teardown

narrative.Action: Create src/cli/commands/__tests__/kill.test.ts. Mock '../../../lib/agents.js' (stopAgent, getAgentState), '../../../lib/tmux.js' (sessionExists), '../../../lib/remote/index.js' (isRemoteAvailable -> { available: false }), '../../../lib/workspace-manager.js' (stopWorkspaceDocker), '../../../lib/projects.js' (resolveProjectFromIssue), and '../../../lib/lifecycle/archive-planning.js' (findWorkspacePath). Follow the close.test.ts vi.hoisted + vi.mock + late-import pattern so killCommand binds to the mocks. Stub process.exit to prevent test-process abort. Cover three behaviors as acceptance subItems below. Tests must run under the default `npm test` config and pass without using real timers (no retries / no backoff are exercised here, so vi.useFakeTimers is not required, but no real setTimeout/sleep calls either).

subItems.title: Specialist case: getAgentState returns { issueId: 'PAN-1052', role: 'ship', workspace: undefined }. killCommand('agent-pan-1052-ship', {}) calls findWorkspacePath(projectPath, 'pan-1052') and stopWorkspaceDocker(<findWorkspacePath's return value>, 'pan-1052'). state.workspace's absence does NOT skip docker teardown.

Work-agent regression case: getAgentState returns { issueId: 'PAN-1052', role: 'work', workspace: '/orchestrator/wrong/path' }. findWorkspacePath is mocked to return '/resolved/by/issue/path'. killCommand calls stopWorkspaceDocker with '/resolved/by/issue/path' (the resolver's output), NOT '/orchestrator/wrong/path' (state.workspace). Asserts on the mock's call arguments, not on side effects.
Guard case: getAgentState returns { issueId: undefined, ... }. stopWorkspaceDocker is NOT called at all (the issueId guard short-circuits the resolver chain). findWorkspacePath also not called.
`npm test -- kill.test.ts` passes locally. No real setTimeout / sleep calls. process.exit is stubbed so failures surface as expect failures, not test-process aborts.

items.title: Extend agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts with route-level docker teardown assertions

narrative.Action: Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts with a new describe block exercising the docker-teardown branch added in commit bd7d00787. Add vi.mock declarations for the SAME specifier strings the route uses inside its dynamic imports — '../../../lib/projects.js' and '../../../lib/lifecycle/archive-planning.js' — and for '../../../../lib/workspace-manager.js' (stopWorkspaceDocker). Add subItem assertions matching the three behaviors below. Reuse the existing runAgentStopHandler helper. The dynamic `await import(...)` inside the route's Effect.promise resolves through vitest's module registry, so static vi.mock declarations DO intercept it as long as the specifiers match exactly.

subItems.title: Specialist case: getAgentStateAsync resolves to { issueId: 'PAN-1052', role: 'ship', workspace: undefined }. Running the stop handler calls resolveProjectFromIssue('PAN-1052'), findWorkspacePath(projectPath, 'pan-1052'), and stopWorkspaceDocker(<findWorkspacePath's return>, 'pan-1052'). state.workspace's absence does NOT skip docker teardown.

Work-agent regression case: getAgentStateAsync resolves to { issueId: 'PAN-1052', role: 'work', workspace: '/orchestrator/wrong/path' }. findWorkspacePath returns '/resolved/by/issue/path'. The handler calls stopWorkspaceDocker with '/resolved/by/issue/path', NOT '/orchestrator/wrong/path'.
Guard case: getAgentStateAsync resolves to { issueId: undefined, ... }. stopWorkspaceDocker is NOT called. findWorkspacePath is NOT called either.
`npm test -- agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts` passes. Existing PAN-1221 F1 lifecycle-event assertions still pass (no regression on the prior test cases).

items.title: Add docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md — dashboard stop verification recipe

narrative.Action: Create docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md as a reproducible playbook the human verifier can run after merge. Capture: prerequisites (a running workspace with docker containers, e.g. start a workspace via `pan start <issueId>` and confirm `docker ps` shows the panopticon-feature-<id>-{frontend,dev} containers); steps (open dashboard, identify a ship/test specialist agent for that issue OR start one if needed, click the stop button); expected before/after evidence (`docker ps` shows containers before, absent after; dashboard logs show `[agents] ✓ Stopped Docker stack for <agentId>`); failure mode to watch for (containers still visible after stop → regression). Reference commit bd7d00787 and the PAN-1326 issue body's test plan. Keep it under ~80 lines.

subItems.title: File docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md exists, is committed, and is linked from the PR description.

Recipe includes: prerequisites, numbered steps, expected before/after `docker ps` evidence, and a 'how to spot a regression' note pointing at the original symptom (specialist kill leaves containers running).
Doc references commit bd7d00787 (the fix) and the original issue test-plan item 'Manual: kill a ship specialist via dashboard, confirm `docker ps` no longer shows that issue's stack'.
